agenda_pressure: Prefer exact internalization over stronger ambient overapproximation results.
Reward characterization, converse, and impossibility theorems that explain verified counterexamples.
Use `list_degree` mainly as a recursion and control parameter, not just as another finiteness bound.
Down-rank local support lemmas unless they directly unlock exact recursion or finite internal recognition.
current_bottlenecks: No theorem yet converts well-founded degree descent into an exact recursive acceptance computation.
There is no canonical exact finite state object extracted from reachable residual states.
Terminal residual states and nonaccepting dead ends are not yet structurally characterized.
The gap between true reachability and the bounded ambient slice is evidenced but not explained conceptually.
desired_summary_changes: An exact backward-recursive acceptance theorem replaces external residual-graph acceptance.
A canonical finite internal automaton or bounded state model captures residual acceptance exactly.
Terminal and dead-end residual states are characterized sharply enough to drive recursion.
The failure of ambient bounded slices is explained by a structural criterion, not only isolated witnesses.
important_verified_counterexamples: Verified counterexample to: For every finite list src and atom s, the class of states p such that p.2 = s and every formula B in p.1 is a subformula of some formula C in src is finite.

Verified counterexample to: Prove a one-sided exactness theorem for ordinary support closure: if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is left-only, then `SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A`; dually, if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is ...
Verified counterexample to: Prove exact completeness of the support closure by showing `∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)`.
missing_bridges: From well-founded residual descent to executable backward recursion on acceptance and rejection.
From exact reachable-set finiteness to a canonical finite internal automaton.
From terminal-state structure to a sharp local acceptance or rejection criterion.
From bounded-slice counterexamples to a criterion separating reachable states from merely admissible ambient states.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize theorems that turn well-founded degree descent and terminal-state structure into an exact bounded recursion or finite internal automaton for residual acceptance.
next_direction.label: exact_finite_internalization
next_direction.rationale: Reachable states are already finite and degree-controlled, while the verified same-atom infinitude and bounded-slice nonexactness results rule out naive ambient-slice exactness.

overexplored_patterns: Repackaging already solved reachable-set or bounded-slice finiteness into explicit finite containers.
Further one-step or multi-step descent restatements without a new recursion or exactness payoff.
Ambient slice lemmas that ignore the verified same-atom infinitude and bounded-slice nonexactness evidence.
Additional isolated nonreachability witnesses without a general boundary criterion.
theory_snapshot: Residualization is now the central exact interface: derivability reduces to atomic residual roots, residual-graph acceptance is exact, and every reachable state preserves the target atom while staying inside the source subformula closure. Structural control is also tight: each residual step strictly decreases `list_degree`, the step relation is well founded, reachable sets are finite, and same-atom subformula families are finite exactly when their `list_degree` is uniformly bounded. The main frontier is exact internal computation, because there is still no canonical backward-recursive acceptance theorem or finite internal recognizer extracted from these facts. Verified boundary evidence matters here: the unrestricted same-atom subformula universe can be infinite, and even the natural degree-bounded same-atom slice can strictly overapproximate true reachability.
